completion_promises.criterion_text: resolve_context_flags() in src/forge/adapters/guardkit/context_resolver.py returns a ResolvedContext with flags, paths, warnings
completion_promises.evidence: src/forge/adapters/guardkit/context_resolver.py lines 83-94 declare `@dataclass(frozen=True) class ResolvedContext` with `flags: list[str]`, `paths: list[str]`, `warnings: list[GuardKitWarning]` (default-factory list per ASSUM-007 stateless contract). Function `resolve_context_flags()` (lines 102-299) constructs and returns this dataclass. Verified by tests/forge/adapters/guardkit/test_context_resolver.py::TestResolvedContextShape::test_resolved_context_has_three_named_attributes (PASSED in this turn's pytest run).

completion_promises.criterion_text: _COMMAND_CATEGORY_FILTER matches DDR-005 verbatim (9 entries — Graphiti subcommands are intentionally absent)
completion_promises.evidence: context_resolver.py lines 49-60 define _COMMAND_CATEGORY_FILTER with exactly 9 keys (system-arch, system-design, system-plan, feature-spec, feature-plan, task-review, task-work, task-complete, autobuild) and category sets matching DDR-005 verbatim. Comment at line 59 documents the deliberate Graphiti omission. Verified by TestCommandCategoryFilterMatchesDDR005 (4 tests including parametrised graphiti-* absence checks for graphiti-search, graphiti-export, graphiti-import, graphiti-prune) — all PASSED.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Missing manifest -> returns empty flags, single GuardKitWarning(code='context_manifest_missing', ...), never raises
completion_promises.evidence: context_resolver.py lines 152-167: when origin manifest path does not exist, _load_manifest returns None, the function appends a single GuardKitWarning(code='context_manifest_missing', message=..., details={'path': ...}) and returns ResolvedContext(flags=[], paths=[], warnings=[warning]) without raising. Verified by TestMissingManifestDegradesGracefully — 3 tests (returns_empty_flags, emits_single_warning, does_not_raise) all PASSED.

completion_promises.criterion_text: internal_docs.always_include paths are prepended to the flag list regardless of category filter
completion_promises.evidence: context_resolver.py lines 172-187 read internal_docs.always_include from origin manifest and validate each path; line 291 starts the ordered_paths list with always_include_paths BEFORE the category-filtered docs are appended (lines 292-293). always_include paths bypass the category filter check entirely (no filter is applied in that loop). Verified by TestAlwaysIncludePrepended — 3 tests (test_always_include_paths_appear_before_category_paths, test_always_include_added_for_uncategorised_doc, test_flags_pair_each_path_with_context_argument) all PASSED.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Dependency chase follows manifests up to depth 2 then stops with context_manifest_cycle_detected warning
completion_promises.evidence: context_resolver.py line 74 sets _DEPTH_CAP=2 (ASSUM-002, manifest hops). The BFS loop (lines 196-286) carries depth in each frame; lines 240-256 emit a GuardKitWarning(code='context_manifest_cycle_detected', details={'reason': 'depth_cap', 'depth_cap': 2, ...}) and skip recursion when depth+1 > _DEPTH_CAP. Verified by TestDepthCapChase — 3 tests (depth_one_chase_collects_dep_doc, depth_two_chase_collects_dep_doc, depth_three_stops_with_depth_cap_warning) all PASSED.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Cycle detection: a manifest already visited in the current chain is not re-visited
completion_promises.evidence: context_resolver.py line 197 initialises the BFS chain as frozenset({origin_manifest_path}); line 283 extends the chain on each recursion (chain | {dep_manifest_path}); lines 257-272 emit GuardKitWarning(code='context_manifest_cycle_detected', details={'reason': 'cycle', ...}) and skip recursion when dep_manifest_path is already in the current chain. Per-chain (not global) tracking allows diamond imports to remain legal. Verified by TestCycleDetection::test_two_node_cycle_resolves_safely (PASSED).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Documents whose resolved absolute path is outside read_allowlist are omitted with a structured warning naming the omitted path
completion_promises.evidence: context_resolver.py lines 372-386 (_validate_doc_path): after the repo-root check, iterates read_allowlist with `_is_within(resolved, allowed)` — if no entry matches, emits GuardKitWarning(code='context_manifest_path_outside_allowlist', message=..., details={'path': abs_path, 'allowlist': [...]}) and returns (False, abs_path) so the doc is omitted from output. Verified by TestReadAllowlistExclusion::test_doc_outside_allowlist_is_omitted_and_warned (PASSED).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Documents whose path resolves outside the repo root are omitted with a structured warning
completion_promises.evidence: context_resolver.py lines 356-370 (_validate_doc_path): performs `_is_within(resolved, owning_repo_root)` BEFORE the allowlist check; if the resolved path escapes the repo (e.g. via `..` or symlink), emits GuardKitWarning(code='context_manifest_path_outside_repo', details={'path': abs_path, 'repo_root': str(owning_repo_root)}) and returns (False, abs_path). Verified by TestDocPathEscapesRepoRoot::test_path_with_dotdot_escape_is_rejected (PASSED).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Resolution is stateless — two concurrent calls against the same repo_path produce independent ResolvedContext values, no module-level cache
completion_promises.evidence: context_resolver.py uses no @functools.cache, no module-level mutable state, and ResolvedContext fields use field(default_factory=list) so every call constructs fresh lists. Each invocation re-reads manifests from disk (lines 152-153, 273). Verified by TestStatelessness::test_two_calls_return_independent_lists which mutates the first result's lists and asserts the second call's lists are independent (PASSED).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Order is stable: always_include first, then categories in _COMMAND_CATEGORY_FILTER insertion order, then by manifest declaration order within each category
completion_promises.evidence: context_resolver.py lines 66-72 declare _CATEGORY_ORDER=('architecture', 'decisions', 'specs', 'contracts', 'source') — first-appearance order across DDR-005 dict values. Lines 291-293 assemble ordered_paths by extending always_include_paths first, then iterating _CATEGORY_ORDER and extending docs_by_category[category] (which preserves BFS+declaration order via list.append in the BFS loop at line 234). Verified by TestStableOrder::test_always_include_then_categories_then_declaration_order with mixed-category fixtures (PASSED).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Symlinks are followed before allowlist check
completion_promises.evidence: context_resolver.py line 353 (_validate_doc_path) calls `candidate.resolve(strict=False)` BEFORE either the repo-root or allowlist checks. Path.resolve() follows symlinks, so a symlink whose target is outside the allowlist is correctly rejected by the subsequent `_is_within` check. Verified by TestSymlinksFollowedBeforeAllowlistCheck::test_symlink_target_outside_allowlist_is_rejected (PASSED on Linux; skipped on Windows where symlinks require admin).
